name: Inter-American Development Bank (IDB)
mandate_summary: Finance and coordinate infrastructure and development platforms across Latin America and the Caribbean.
website_notes: Identity = Pre-FID infrastructure structuring authority aligned with sovereign infrastructure deployment and multilateral capital formation across Latin America.
Identification = Commitment architecture authority that resolves sovereign capital stack ambiguity before MDB project preparation and financing approval.
Access Physics = Sovereign borrower alignment + regional development capital structuring relevance.
Distribution Mechanics = Precision routing through sovereign ministries, regional infrastructure programs, and MDB-aligned structuring channels.
Conversion Physics = Collapse capital stack ambiguity -> enable project preparation approval -> secure structuring mandate before FID.
transfer_hypothesis: When sovereign or regional infrastructure platforms cannot reach bankability due to capital stack and governance ambiguity, USG can define commitment architecture before project preparation approval, enabling pre-FID structuring mandates and de-risked MDB-linked deployment.

system_dynamics: Shielding: HIGH — institutional gatekeeping.
Decision Speed: SLOW — sovereign + MDB review cycles.
Risk Exposure: HIGH — sovereign lending and development risk.
Altitude Sensitivity: CRITICAL — sovereign-to-MDB alignment required.
Channel Dependence: VERY HIGH — formal institutional entry channels.

secondary: Infrastructure platform architecture memo routed through sovereign ministry
Regional infrastructure modernization briefing

conversion_steps: Step 1 — Platform Pressure: A sovereign or regional infrastructure platform exists but cannot reach bankability due to capital stack or governance ambiguity.
Step 2 — Platform Framing: Define platform scope, capital stack architecture, FID boundary, and governance structure.
Step 3 — Structural Alignment: Confirm structural prerequisites required before MDB project preparation and financing approval.
Step 4 — Mandate Execution: Secure Pre-FID infrastructure structuring mandate tied to platform architecture.
Step 5 — FID Trigger: Platform becomes bankable; capital deployment begins; MDB financing activates.

name: Tariq Al Futtaim Family Foundation (TAFF)
mandate_summary: Deploy family capital into long-term strategic platforms that strengthen Gulf economic positioning and cross-regional infrastructure.
description: TAFF operates as a generational capital allocator with the ability to sponsor multi-actor platforms linking Gulf demand with emerging-market infrastructure systems. The foundation has direct access to UAE regulatory, logistics, and commercial networks and can anchor capital for corridor platforms connecting the Gulf with Africa.
website_notes: Identity: Pre-FID Infrastructure Structuring Authority aligned to family-capital platform deployment.
Identification: Platform architect designing corridor governance, capital stack, and commitment architecture.
Access Physics: Family-to-family trust network + direct mandate authority.
Distribution Mechanics: Trusted relationship channel through Abdulraouf and the Al-Futtaim network.
Conversion Physics: Collapse structural ambiguity -> formalize mandate -> platform architecture -> FID-ready corridor.

system_dynamics: Shielding: Medium — family governance structure but accessible through trusted relationships.
Decision Speed: Moderate — faster than sovereign funds but still deliberate.
Risk Exposure: High — family reputation and long-term capital preservation.
Altitude Sensitivity: High — must operate at platform architecture level, not execution.
Channel Dependence: High — relationship-based entry rather than institutional processes.

secondary: UAE strategic development alignment
Regional trade corridor initiatives

transfer_chain.primary_infrastructure_pressure: Gulf demand for reliable African supply corridors.
transfer_chain.linked_signals_30d: Dangote refinery export ramp; Gulf retail demand growth; Middle East energy volatility
transfer_chain.strategic_alignment_thesis: A Gulf-Africa corridor platform strengthens the UAE’s position as a global trade hub while securing supply chains for key commodities.
transfer_chain.required_governance_artifacts: Corridor platform architecture memo; capital stack structure; SPV/JV governance model; supply anchor agreements
transfer_chain.critical_blocker: Refinery export allocations stabilizing.

top_signals: Middle East refinery disruptions increasing product scarcity

mandate_readiness.upgrade_readiness: High once corridor platform structure presented
